column3: Sometimes there is a bit of tension here and there, but so far have been able to get through it.
The report took a long time for us to complete mainly based on the the process control that Raine was responsible with. Based on circumstances it was difficult for Raine to finish her part on time.
No complaints really. The team works well together and produces fantastic work.
TO should've been a first ballot Hall of Famer.

Would enjoy speakers that describe how they work in a team effectively; although engineering skills are essential, being able to work in a team is the most critical component in any type of job that's collaborative. Often times, teammates may forget/be unaware of the consequences or hinderance to progress that result from deviating from schedules.

Eric and Alex seem to work more on process control than on senior design. Although I agree both classes are very important, our reports so far have been very subpar and I am not able to pick up on the slack myself. I simply do not know how to work with Aspen and can't help with sizing equipment. I will still try to be as diligent as I can with my work but I can't help but feel disillusioned and demoralized with this project.
Everyone works well together. We're united in doing the best we can and have spent several over-nights at school working on our design report and presentation.

Assalamualaikum. Respected professor, I was expecting few lectures periods to review some chapters from past semester. But, most of the time you helped students and mentored them about their project, which indeed has helped everyone.

For this report I decided to cover the process control section and was later asked to cover sizing the electrolysis unit for our new design basis and find the new equipment cost. A lot of my time and work went into research for this section and unifying that with the conditions we had set already.
